paula.bio:
Г°ВџВ‡В©Г°ВџВ‡ВҒ Ich bereite dich auf die B1-PrГѓВәfung vor.
Г°ВџВҺВҮAlles fГѓВәr B1 - einfach erklГѓВ¤rt!
Г°ВџВЏВ† 19 Jahre Erfahrung |15000+ Teilnehmende
Г°ВџВ”ВӨ Grammatik-CrashcourseГ°ВџВ‘В‡
www.language-progress.com/crashcourse
paula.authority: The subject doesn't show herself as a teacher, she more so blends with the topic itself by getting into characters and using no extended introductions to videos. She rather presents the language itself.
paula.quality: The German is mostly correct but phonetically flawed. She is still above-average compared to other teachers and German speakers I heard speak in Poland. Considering her posting frequency however and briefness of the content itself I think that it's more of a promo tool than tangible help for learners.
paula.psr: Her acting is subliminally quirky. The vulnerable struggles played can be very relatable for viewers. She sits in a hotel room in a bathrobe in the sink reel which can seem very intimate.
paula.selling: It is seperated. Not even her reels' descriptions point at her product. She hopes for sales through her profile instead of the content pieces directly.
paula.freeform: I have to say that she does make a very competent impression and I would even say she, to a certain extent, does a pretty solid job. But the task that she has given herself is very easy and I don't see a big work ethic behind it. Not to call her lazy. The videos themselves sometimes show production quality because she even stood at a real receptionist desk in a fitting outfit. Her accent is probably only noticeable to real native speakers, because the stress placements and some endings or phoneme connections are what exposes her. If I were a German learner I would have the impression that she is perfect.
paula.r1_notes: She starts the reel simply by saying "A1 kontra B1". It's okay she wants to jump right in, however for me as a native speaker the term "kontra" sounds odd. I personally would have used the term 'gegen' or even 'versus' despite the fact that it's an anglicism, even though I would understand caution with non-german vocab, I merely want to note it'd be at least natural sounding. The following terminology she presents e.g. fragen -> sich erkundigen is useful and a good idea, but the terms she compares are often only associated but misleading to be taken as exact replacer. For example she presents B1 'GebГѓВ¤ude' as upgrade to A1 'Haus', but one needs to understand the broader context and it makes it all a bit of a slippery slope in my perception. Her pronunciation is quite okay, but flawed, and she wrongly pronounces the -ig ending in southern German style which I find problematic because she should teach Hochdeutsch.
paula.r2_notes: I like the idea and I have to say even though she is not sovereign as an actress (too much hesitation in character when ordering food) it is a helpful reel to learners. I have to note though that she said "Ich hГѓВ¤tt' gerne" which is very odd, because a native speaker would either articulate the entire sentence cleanly or if elliptic they would say "Ich hГѓВ¤tte gern", but not the variation she presented - it may exist but I feel like it is way less common.
paula.r3_notes: The reel is super short and contains minimal teaching, it's simply "ich werde trinken", "ich trinke", "ich habe getrunken" which gives her little room for error. Her pronunciation is semi-alright again, meaning decent but with accent.
paula.r4_notes: She presents the words beiГѓВџen, kauen, schmatzen, ausspucken. I don't like her pronunciation of schmatzen, she widens the "a" and shortens the "tz". She stresses it /'schma,tzn/.
paula.r5_notes:
The reel itself is quite good, criticizing this one would be harsh. Above her usual performance. In this one however the description is flawed. 

"Wie bitte um die Rechnung? Г°ВџВ’ВіГ°ВџВ‡В©Г°ВџВ‡ВҒ

In diesem Reel zeige ich dir 3 verschiedene MГѓВ¶glichkeiten [...]"

Incomplete sentence, subject is lacking.
paula.r6_notes:
Pronunciation is alright with occasional stress placement faults.
Vocab presented is correct.
On-screen caption says "schГѓВ¶ne beschreung" - Noun wrongly put in lowercase!
paula.r7_notes: Nothing to correct. Very plain and flat reel that correctly corrects the grammar in 5 sentences.
paula.r8_notes: She plays a character struggling to find the right words for "the sink is clogged". In the end of the reel she plays the receptionist saying "Das Waschbecken ist verstopft". Honestly I find this reel alright and helpful.

ania.bio:
рџ‡©рџ‡Ғ UczД™ dorosЕ‚ych mГіwiД‡ po niemiecku
рџ’­ Potocznie. Naturalnie. Bez chaosu i stresu.
вқЊ Niemiecki nie chce ruszyД‡? OdkrД™cimy to!
в¤№ Zapis na nasze lekcje
linktr.ee/ania_germania
ania.authority: Same as Paula Boinska she doesn't really. She goes straight to the point however in her case she does a little polish opener hook before to tell her viewers what the reel will be about.
ania.quality: Similarly, her content sometimes leans useful but mostly lacks structure. I doubt that a learner would seriously benefit from spending time on her reels.
ania.psr: Eye contact, smile, camera angles look consciously picked for attractiveness, perhaps authority. She uses (probably) fictional stories to tell a narrative (e.g. "I have just been scammed! This is how I'm feeling now!").
ania.selling: No mention of products in the content pieces. Only in-post CTAs encourages to follow (end sentences). The linktree with her products is only found in her bio.
ania.freeform: I liked her more than Paula Boinska because she comes across as less tryhard serious, her smile reads as more genuine and she hesitates way less, she actually pulls through with her sentences in instant fashion, even while still a bit flawed or wrong she owns them with confidence.
ania.r1_notes:
Her accent is also Polish but it's not the least pleasant. However she does this typical uvular trill [КЂ], like many Poles. It's off-putting.
The vocab presented is correct, however I found the prepositions she used odd. I'm not sure if my gut feeling is right, but her presented "Ich sehe schlecht in der Ferne" would sound better to me if it was "Ich sehe schlecht AUS der Ferne".
Her stress-placement leaves room for adjustment, especially since in the beginning she says [Ich bin 'kurzsichtig] but later in the video she says [Ich bin 'weit'sichtig] (double stress). That's definitely not correct to teach.
ania.r2_notes:
This reel was made as a trap song, with an altered voice saying different german sentences like "Ich mache mein Bett", put on a loop (one repetition per sentence). The musical production completely distracts from pronunciation markers, but since I have to judge it I would say these ones are pretty smooth.
The ironic thing is that in this comment section people started correcting her Polish translations as wrong, for example commenting "W co ubierasz piЕәamД™?", implementing wrong Polish grammar even though she is a native speaker of Polish.
ania.r3_notes: The author presents the sentences "Ich kippe gleich um", "Ich muss mal an die Frische Luft" and "Ich muss mal runterkommen". The sentences hit the right tone when it comes to casualness and authenticity and the pronunciation is great, with the lone exception that she formed [luЛђft] instead of the correct [lКЉft].
ania.r4_notes: Collab reel with the creator @deutsch.mit.lucy. She presents different German vocabulary, and Lucy presents the Austrian counterpart for each. Nothing to correct apart from their usual imperfect pronunciations.
ania.r5_notes: The video presents colloquial ways of expressing being busy and having a lot on one's mind. There is one drastic mistake in this video at the very last expression. The author says "Bei mir ist die HГәlle los", thinking she says "Bei mir ist die HГ¶lle los". This wrong vowel formation creates a different noun and misleads the audience.
ania.r6_notes: The author presents 4 ways of expressing anger - namely 'stinksauer' 'genervt', 'wГәtend' and 'angepisst'. Actually she is very on point in this video, the only thing is that I would translate 'genervt' to 'zdenerwowany' instead of her translation choice of 'poirytowany'.
ania.r7_notes: The video presents the expressions "Nicht meine Baustelle", "nicht mein Bier", "nicht mein Zirkus, nicht meine Affen". I only know nicht mein Bier, I have never stumbled across the remaining ones, so I am unable to judge those.
ania.r8_notes: The video starts with the hook "These 3 sentences will evolve your German". It's a gray area. The claim is perhaps undisprovable, but in most cases a massive exaggeration. Conscious or unconscious persuasion strategy suspicion. Also the sentences presented are "mir nichts, dir nichts", ['SpaГџ 'beiseite] and "BlГ¶d gelaufen". Dumm gelaufen is more common so she should have in my gut feeling presented that one instead. And as just noted, the stress placement is too active, she doesn't hit the notes with it. Also in total the expressions are colloquial, authentic and useful but unrelated with one another which makes this reel seem so random, especially considering her opening hook - it makes it sound a little silly and harder to take serious.

patrycja.bio:
Entrepreneur [PROFILE CATEGORY TAG IN INSTA UI]
рџ‡©рџ‡ҒNiemiecki krok po kroku
рџ‘©рџЏәвЂҚрџ’»Mieszkam w DE, uczД™ praktycznie i skutecznie
рџ“љ5500+ osГіb korzysta z moich materiaЕ‚Гіw
рџ¤°рџЏәGrupa + e-book ciД…Еәa w DE
www.jezykniemiecki-dlakazdego.edu.pl/linki
patrycja.authority: Her bio mentions that she lives in Germany, as if that had any meaning. An immigrant who never learns the language can also claim to live there, it's not an authority marker. Also she claims in her bio to "teach practically and effectively" which is stupid because it should be obvious for a teacher and otherwise she shouldn't teach anyways, it's awkward that she uses such wording, she lost my authority so quickly, right off the bat.
patrycja.quality: Because she relies on TTS obviously it's an easy way of avoiding errors. But it lacks personality. Her instagram profile alone presents close to zero value for learners. It's all a setup for her money-making products.
patrycja.psr: In this one I am shocked by the sheer lack of any relationship building.
patrycja.selling: The marketing is very pushy and the language content looks like a supplement to her self-promoting posts rather than the other way around...
patrycja.freeform: I have only analyzed the first 3 reels because the older ones are based on the same concept. I find this channel to come across as even lazier than the previous ones. I don't understand the audiovisual choices, I find them badly placed, confusing, it's one big chaos. Also she is aggressive with her e-book promo. Every reel has it advertised both on-screen, as well as in the reel's description. She has hidden her likes because they are very low, she likes her own content probably because she believes it will boost her or that she can compensate that way, and in general we never see her face in the videos. Her name is also so generic "german for everyone", what a lazy and overconfident claim, she presents herself like this one-fits-all problem solver but honestly offers only shit. It's a terrible profile and I'd find it immoral if she earned a living off this.
patrycja.r1_notes:
It's a self-promotion reel that presents a web page where one can practice with Polish-German flashcards. The caption above says "PREMIERA NOWEGO E-BOOKA! ZOBACZ JAK WYGLД„DA PRACA Z FISZKAMI - GRY / WYMOWA / NAUKA".
I am confused because I cannot understand what it really is that she is promoting. For me an e-book is merely a virtual book, the interactive app being something different. I feel like she is promoting two things at once and it is very unclear to me what it is really about. The app presented on the screen recording is computer-generated TTS audio, reading the German sentences accent-free but obviously synthetically.
patrycja.r2_notes:
I noticed she likes her own content.
This is a video with Abba's money money money song and simply the words 'die KrГ¶te', 'die Kohle', 'der Zaster', die 'Maus'. It's weird to me she didn't say "die MГ¤use" instead as plural - her pick is either incorrect or I have not stumbled upon somebody using it in singular to express having money colloquially.
The reel is only text with music... At the end her e-book premiere is announced. I think this gets less to the people than the talking head videos of the previous creators (my assumption).
patrycja.r3_notes: The video has a b-roll looped woman in the background and on the higher layer there are 4 german idiots relating urging somebody to talk, with their polish counterparts spelled out below. The German sentences are on an oval yellow background and the Polish on a blue one. Visually it is already insane and super hard to focus on, but the worst part about this reel is that she put "Tam sЕ‚oЕ„ce, gdzie my", a generic mainstream trending pop song by Wiktor DyduЕ‚a over the reel which I find stupid. When you're trying to teach people German you shouldn't let a person sing in a different language into their ears while they have to read and comprehend text in a foreign language. It's distracting, absurd and cringeworthy.

lucy.bio:
в‡ЁPomogД™ ci zaczД…Д‡ mГіwiД‡ po niemieckuрџ‡©рџ‡Ғ
в‡ЁTysiД…ce osГіb juЕә mi zaufaЕ‚o
в­ђпёЏPonad 15 lat praktyki w nauczaniu
в¤№ ZACZNIJ TUTAJрџ‡©рџ‡Ғ
linktr.ee/deutsch.mit.lucy
lucy.authority: Her red outfit and minimalistic but stylish background are visually appealing. She has the biggest screen presence so far and the best developed presentation confidence. She places CTAs in her reels that are more extensive than usual creators do, because sometimes she puts 2 or 3 different ones, but they are all related. She simply takes much room for them while being charming and confident and persuasive. From a business and performer standpoint she does that damn well. Also there are some reels of hers where she speaks very collectedly and with a conscious, slower pace - rhetorically that makes her seem super smart and authorative, like she's making herself impossible to doubt. But she doesn't do so in an arrogant, overconfident way - gladly she keeps her charm and friendliness!
lucy.quality: Language-wise she is the most accurate so far. I haven't noticed a single error and the only thing I would recommend her to approve is to be laser-sharp when it comes to vowel formation, to reduce her accent even more, but this is very demanding feedback from me, she is perhaps also the first creator where I'm gravitating more towards a positive impression than a negative one. However, I am still not convinced about the value provided, I think it takes much more to leave me impressed. It's still more of quicktips than an actual continuous stream of educational value - but perhaps analyzing that in this text field would open too big of a theoretical question if this isn't the maximum of what a creator should provide on Instagram as a medium to not overload the audience (compared to external mediums like video courses or e-books).
lucy.psr: She direcly adresses the viewers and talks to you like she's already your friend. She uses casual language that makes her appear relatable.
lucy.selling: Some posts are only promoting her book, some talk about cultural/religious aspects related to Germany (e.g. Easter), and also her reels feel very everyday, with the abbreviations. She simply presents to people how to REALLY sound German and honestly she doesn't come across as big of an impostor.
lucy.freeform: I really want to note that so far she has the biggest screen presence and speaking confidence, and even language-wise she is the most accurate so far. I haven't noticed a single error and the only thing I would recommend her to approve is to be laser-sharp when it comes to vowel formation, to reduce her accent even more, but this is very demanding feedback from me, she is perhaps also the first creator where I'm gravitating more towards a positive impression than a negative one. She has a little bit of this boss appeal/sexappeal, she is decently attractive rhetorically and optically.
lucy.r1_notes: This is a full-scale video ad with b-rolls, her voice over and a book presented. She reads an advertizing script taylored at telling the viewer that they need her book to finally "reach her destination", which is knowing better German. The entire post is in Polish only.
lucy.r2_notes: In this reel the author sits in a classy red outfit and a minimalistic background (designer lamp + stack of her own book) and talks to the camera. The majority of the video is Polish, with the exception of the phrases "Ich bin 32 Jahre alt" and "Ich bin 32". The author explains to the audience that in everyday speech it is completely fine to leave out the last part of the sentence for ease, without losing correctness.
lucy.r3_notes: The hook is in Polish, as again the majority of the reel. The author teaches how to use "ehrlich gesagt" when underlining honesty. Her phonetics are decent, with a little accent that leaves me unhappy. The video ends with a tip from the author, suggesting to write down the sentence learnt in order to repeat and remember it. Finally, she does a friendly CTA urging to follow her for more.
lucy.r4_notes: We can leave the exact same feedback as I gave in reel #3, because I noticed that there are jumpcuts with transitions around the sequence with the actual phrase, meaning that for workflow efficiency and consistancy she reuses the already recorded bits and merely replaces the phrase being presented for a new one. In this reel that is "Das hГ¤tte ich nicht erwartet". The Polish translation "tego siД™ nie spodziewaЕ‚am" is acceptable.
lucy.r5_notes: In this reel the author starts with a very casual, humoristic imperative order to "throw out the letter E" in order to sound more German. The phonetic process that she points at is elision - saying "Ich hab'" instead of "Ich habe". This is actually a very practical tip and I find it great that she presented it because she doesn't exaggerate the impact it has. Video ends with very friendly CTAs. She uses the word "zostaw serduszko" when asking for a like, which is a very warm and intimate word to be used.
lucy.r6_notes: This one is actually not a reel, but a caroussel post, possibly Canva designed. The title page explains what the post is about - Easter vocabulary. The following slides are German sentences related to Easter and spring, with the corresponding Polish translations. This post doesn't show her face, only text on a background.
lucy.r7_notes: This is another carousel post like the previously analyzed one. It is arranged in the same way with the thematic difference that this one critically comments on the FUDOG acronym (fГәr um durch ohne gegen) which is a trick to remember dativ and akkusativ connections better. The post criticizes this trick because despite its possible efficiency in memorization helpful for written tests, they don't work in live spoken German, thus it can be a trap to only rely on such tricks. I like this post because it brings awareness to something I would agree with but haven't yet thought about from such an angle. I wasn't aware of the acronym because I don't need it as a native speaker, but the concept is also to be found in other languages or even subjects like biology, even if the handling of such subjects differs.
lucy.r8_notes: This reel features the author's face again. It presents common German abbreviations like z.B., d.h., ca. among others. Quite useful again, even if again more of a dose of terminology than a linguistic theoretical explanation. Pronunciation is decent but not accent-free. Stress placement is a bit off (minimally, still better than in previous creators). And I would also say her vowels are a bit too short. For example the later E in "Beispiel". I as a native speaker word really drag the E in "-spiel" long. But once again I want to note she is better then the previous creators.

olivia.bio:
Language school [INSTAGRAM CATEGORY TAG]
рџљЂ Skutecznie nauczД™ CiД™ niemieckiego
в­ђпёЏ Ekspertka od pokonania bariery jД™zykowej
рџҺ“ 15 lat w DE | +1000 klientГіw
в¬‡пёЏ Zapisz siД™ do FLEX
szprechaj.pl and 2 other links
olivia.r1_topic: Everyday phrases claimed to be useful
olivia.r1_notes: First thing I noticed is that she put an English-speaking song over the reel, and it's even a fast rap track so there's a lot of words making it hard to concentrate on the on-screen captions in German and Polish. Stupid decision. The creator stands in front of the camera covering almost the entire frame with her, which I like, it's a well-picked composition. She has this blonde beauty-look and is decently attractive which can be both a follower magnet AND a distraction from the educational content in the worst case. A creator must be cautious about how much attention they draw to themselves because it's not a beauty contest or personal profile where she can flex like a queen. The educational theme of this reel is that she writes "useful", as she claims, everyday phrases in German. The caption is changed in quick fashion and she snaps her fingers to the rhytm of the text. The first phrase is "du nervst total" which is honestly a bit off-putting and despite it's natural and useful nature I instantly felt a bit harassed as a viewer, this is worth noting.
olivia.r2_notes: I feel confirmed in my previous feeling. In this reel the author presents self-claimed "Red flags in men". The video is targeted only to women and tells them to "run" whenever a man on a date says one of the presented sentences. For example "warum bist du noch single?" is presented and translated to Polish, framed as a red flag, pointing at implied deceitfulness from the male side. I find this reel super problematic. It seems like she is projecting her own feras and insecurities and frustration onto her instagram. One comment criticizes her for exactly that, asking rhetorically whether she hosts a language channel or a dating channel. I agree with the criticicm and I feel repelled.
olivia.r3_notes: In this reel the author claims that whoever understands the following text holds the A2 level in German. I would be very cautious with the claim, it seems very random and self-proclaimed. The following text she speaks rather fluently, with no hesitations, but she has an accent that exposes her and makes me doubt her native speaker status. Especially, as previous creators, vowel formation is too short and for example the T in "Karte" she produced wasn't dental, but closer to palatal.
olivia.r4_notes: She put Sean Paul as music and she dances and makes sexy gestures with her hands. Her outfit is so casual one can see her belly button piercing.
